---
title: "Software Setup"
---

## Core Tools

The course assumes access to:

- [Quarto](https://quarto.org/) for rendering documents and websites;
- `R` for simulation, plotting, diagnostics, and `cmdstanr` workflows;
- Python 3.11 or newer for `JAX`;
- `CmdStan` if you want to run the Stan labs locally.

## Local Setup

### Quarto

Install Quarto, then render the site with:

```bash
quarto render
```

### `R`

Install the required `R` packages with:

```bash
Rscript install.R
```

If you also want `CmdStan`, run:

```bash
INSTALL_CMDSTAN=true Rscript install.R
```

### Python / `JAX`

Install Python dependencies with:

```bash
python -m pip install -r requirements.txt
```

## Cloud Setup

Recommended cloud pathways:

- Posit Cloud for `R` and `Stan`;
- Binder for lightweight JAX examples;
- Codespaces for the full repository-backed experience.

## Lab File Locations

| Language | Path | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `R` | `labs/r/beta_binomial.R` | Conjugate updating and posterior prediction |
| `R` | `labs/r/conjugate_families.R` | Exact posterior updates across conjugate families |
| `R` | `labs/r/laplace_importance_sampling.R` | Laplace approximation and importance sampling |
| `R` | `labs/r/mh_sampler.R` | Random-walk Metropolis implementation |
| `R` | `labs/r/decision_rules.R` | Posterior risk and Bayes estimators |
| `R` | `labs/r/prior_predictive_logistic.R` | Prior predictive simulation for logistic regression |
| `R` | `labs/r/meanfield_vi_normal_gamma.R` | Mean-field CAVI for a Normal-Gamma model |
| `R` | `labs/r/particle_filter_local_level.R` | Bootstrap particle filter for a local-level model |
| `R` | `labs/r/bayesian_linear_regression.R` | Closed-form Bayesian linear regression and prediction |
| `R` | `labs/r/glm_logistic_poisson.R` | Logistic and Poisson posterior building blocks |
| `R` | `labs/r/hierarchical_partial_pooling.R` | Partial-pooling shrinkage geometry |
| `R` | `labs/r/mixture_label_switching.R` | Allocation updates and label switching |
| `R` | `labs/r/gaussian_process_regression.R` | Exact Gaussian-process posterior prediction |
| `R` | `labs/r/hmm_forward_algorithm.R` | Forward recursion for a hidden Markov model |
| `R` | `labs/r/causal_sensitivity_grid.R` | Treatment-effect sensitivity grid |
| `Stan` | `labs/stan/eight_schools.stan` | Canonical hierarchical model |
| `R` | `labs/r/fit_eight_schools.R` | `cmdstanr` wrapper for the Stan lab |
| Python/JAX | `labs/jax/jax_logistic_map_hmc.py` | Autodiff, MAP, and basic HMC-style demo |
| Python/JAX | `labs/jax/hmc_gaussian_demo.py` | HMC geometry on a correlated Gaussian target |

## Reproducibility Advice

- Fix random seeds in all stochastic examples.
- Record package versions for each course release.
- Keep model files and wrapper scripts in separate language-specific folders.
- Prefer explicit data preparation code over hidden preprocessing.
